results.title: Gemini 3.5 Live Translate: Latest audio model for live speech-to-speech translation | Product Hunt
results.content:
Promoted

Steven Berg

Degraded speech patterns, variable speaking rate, vocal fatigue, and noisy environments all stand in the way of finding invariant speech forms across naturally spoken signals. I wonder how new technologies will help us overcome these barriers to fully understanding speech perception and spoken word recognition. Perhaps tools like Gemini's Live Translate will help us identify additional cues that we rely on for language perception which have been eluding empirical investigators in scientific research(?). Perhaps not.

Report

Xuefei Mei

If Gemini 3.5 handles those edge cases well, it changes what's possible for multilingual meetings. How does the system handle speakers with heavy accents or non-native pronunciation patterns?

Report

Jared Salois

## Make your product AI-native via AI Copilot

322 followers

## Make your product AI-native via AI Copilot

Visit website

After 100+ conversations with app builders, the same thing kept coming up: Everyone wants an AI copilot in their product. No one has time to wire it up. So we built Crow to add a chat-first copilot that can take real actions on any product within minutes.

📌 [...] Report

Ohans Emmanuel

Remention

Remention

Congrats on the launch! Access to BE endpoints on one hand means the agent is powerful and can make useful tool calls, but not without risks. As a malicious user, can't do funny things?   
  
Is the onus on the provider of the endpoints to add grade A security, or does Crow add a proxy in the middle to handle things like rate limiting (so the endpoint isn't spammed either by a malicious user or LLM gone rogue), strong schema validation etc.?

If you manage to add a proxy, it can change the perception of users who are more on the cautious side i.e., some extra confidence beyond the JWT auth

Report

Jai Bhatia

Crow

Crow

Maker

@ohansemmanuel Great point! [...] Report

Masum Parvej

@jai\_bhatia2 I’m curious how you handle security and permissions. If the agent can execute API calls, making sure it doesn’t overstep or expose sensitive actions seems like a critical next step.

Report

Jai Bhatia

Crow

Crow

Maker

@masump Good question. The agent never bypasses your auth.

We verify every request using your existing JWT/session and a shared secret, so each tool call is authorized exactly like a normal user action. If a user can’t do something in your app, the agent can’t either.

You also control access with a strict tool allow-list, so the agent only sees the endpoints you explicitly permit.

Report

Brent Kom

@jai\_bhatia2 Really clean UI! I like how simple the workflow feels. Curious—what gap did you see in the market that inspired this?

results.title: TimeAlign: Align your time with your goals & optimize your life | Product Hunt
results.content:
Report

Matty Reed

TimeAlign

TimeAlign

Maker

@m4jiz the relevant piece is React Native. This is JavaScript framework for building mobile apps for iOS and Android (and can also be used for desktop apps). This basically means we can use the same code for iOS and Android.

Report

Jordan J. Dominguez

TimeAlign

TimeAlign

Maker

@m4jiz Thank you! and re: "has room for tons of new features" we totally agree - we see this V1.0 as the foundational piece dialing the core feedback loop in. From here... the possibilities have incredible potential. We can layer SO much upon this deeper understanding of what we actually did vs. what we intended. Let us know some of the features you're dreaming up! We also have a public roadmap that you can submit features to or upvote ones that you like. [...] Report

Nft Boss

@jordan\_dominguez ok

Report

Majid Izadi

ToyPal

ToyPal

Gratz on the launch, very cool product, good job by you and your team, liked the UI/UX, has room for tons of new features, when are you expecting the android version?

Report

Matty Reed

TimeAlign

TimeAlign

Maker

@m4jiz Hi Majid, thanks for the kind words! Android will be out shortly - we built on a tech stack that lets us use the same core codebase for both, we just wanted to focus on iOS first. If you'd like to get notified when we release on Android (and even help beta test it for us!) you can sign up here: 

Report

Majid Izadi

ToyPal

ToyPal

@matty\_reed great, what is your tech stack if I may know?

Report

Matty Reed

TimeAlign

TimeAlign

Maker [...] creating a better mobile calendar experience. We are also currently building some of the tracking automations you mentioned (siri integration, auto-categorization, etc) and they should be available in the near future. - Differentiation: this is something we've been working on honing for a long time, so you're feedback is well received. We talked about filming a "day in the life" to show the feature story a bit better. I agree that the messaging can be simplified more and focus on the uniqueness of the product. We will have this messaging (and a nice video) dialed-in by the next launch!
